name: Nellis AFB
profile: Military-heavy Las Vegas housing snapshot anchored by Nellis AFB, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews with affordability, rent, ownership, and military-fit context.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Cooling but still active; buyers have more leverage than during peak pandemic-era conditions.

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 VA-style purchase assumptions and Las Vegas ownership cost estimates.

notes: Nevada property taxes are generally lower than Texas-style markets, which can help all-in monthly ownership cost.
HOA exposure can vary materially in Las Vegas subdivisions and should be included in true payment comparisons.
Insurance, utilities, summer cooling, and commute patterns should be stress-tested before using purchase price alone as the affordability screen.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: Estimated VA loan all-in payment for Las Vegas area using average home value, 6.2% APR, property tax, insurance, and HOA assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Secure military installation environment with controlled access and major Air Force mission presence.
crime_status.metro_las_vegas: Urban crime patterns vary sharply by neighborhood; buyers should compare North Las Vegas, Summerlin, Henderson, and east-side corridors at the neighborhood level.

school_quality.on_base: Military families typically evaluate nearby Clark County School District options and private/charter alternatives depending on commute and housing location.
school_quality.off_base: School quality varies widely across the Las Vegas metro; Summerlin, Henderson, and selected northwest pockets are often stronger family-screening areas.

rental_vacancy.notes: Las Vegas rental market appears more balanced than peak-pandemic conditions, with rents cooling from prior highs but still elevated versus pre-pandemic levels.

rental_metrics.notes: RentCast July 2026 market data shows a balanced rental market across selected Nellis PCS housing ZIPs, with median rent near $1,599.

special_events: Major concerts and conventions on the Las Vegas Strip

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Nellis AFB is a major Air Force hub with advanced combat training, strong military infrastructure, and access to the broader Las Vegas metro housing market.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Las Vegas is more expensive than many traditional military metros, but lower property taxes and cooling home prices can improve the ownership equation for disciplined buyers.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Cooling prices and longer days on market may create negotiation opportunities, especially away from premium west-side and Henderson pockets.

market_bluf.bluf_headline: Cooling Desert Market With Real Leverage
market_bluf.bluf_summary: Nellis families get access to a large, liquid Las Vegas housing market where prices have softened, but all-in ownership still requires careful BAH, commute, HOA, and summer utility stress-testing.

summary_points: Nellis AFB sits inside the Las Vegas metro, a large and liquid housing market with broader options than many military towns.
Home values have cooled year-over-year, which improves buyer leverage compared with peak-market conditions.
Las Vegas remains more expensive than lower-cost military metros, so BAH-to-payment discipline matters.
Lower Nevada property taxes can help offset higher purchase prices, but HOA, insurance, and summer cooling costs still matter.
The best PCS strategy is to compare commute, school quality, HOA exposure, and resale flexibility rather than chasing the lowest list price.
opportunities: Cooling prices and longer days on market can give PCS buyers room to negotiate.
Large metro inventory gives military families more neighborhood choice than isolated base markets.
Lower Nevada property tax rates can support ownership math compared with higher-tax states.
Military-connected demand near Nellis and Creech supports long-term rental relevance.
Single-family rental demand can be durable if the property is located in a practical commute corridor.
risks: Purchase prices remain high enough that some BAH profiles may be better served by renting.
Summer utility costs can materially increase the true monthly housing burden.
HOA dues are common in many Las Vegas subdivisions and should not be ignored.
Neighborhood quality, schools, commute time, and crime exposure vary sharply across the metro.
Premium areas such as Summerlin and Henderson may stretch military affordability faster than North Las Vegas or east/northeast corridors.
buyer_notes: Nellis buyers should evaluate all-in monthly cost, not just the principal-and-interest mortgage estimate.
A lower property tax environment helps, but the Las Vegas purchase price, HOA, insurance, and cooling costs still need to fit inside a safe housing ratio.
North Las Vegas and northeast corridors may improve commute practicality, while Summerlin and Henderson may offer stronger lifestyle appeal at higher prices.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who negotiate, inspect carefully, and avoid emotional overextension.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against the current cooling market rather than old peak-price expectations.
Move-in-ready condition, realistic pricing, and strong presentation matter more when buyers have more inventory to compare.
Homes that serve military commute patterns and family needs can still stand out if positioned correctly.
investor_angles: Nellis and Creech military turnover can support rental demand, but investors should underwrite vacancy and maintenance conservatively.
The Las Vegas market may favor stable cash-flow discipline over aggressive appreciation assumptions.
Single-family homes with practical commute access, moderate HOA exposure, and durable tenant demand are stronger underwriting candidates.
Bedroom-specific rent and purchase bands are more useful than broad metro averages when evaluating military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare HOA, insurance, utilities, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not assume Las Vegas affordability from purchase price alone; summer cooling and HOA can change the answer.
A slightly lower purchase price or lower-HOA property can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near the Nellis and Creech corridors.
Investors should model vacancy, repairs, HOA, insurance, utilities, and turnover conservatively.
The strongest rental candidates are practical homes with good commute access and manageable monthly carrying costs.

market.market_type_summary: Cooling but still active; more buyer leverage than peak market.

base_profile.market_label: Las Vegas / Nellis Military Housing Market
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Nellis AFB is a major combat training and test installation supporting advanced air combat training, Red Flag, mission support, and access to the broader Las Vegas housing market.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Large-metro, desert, mission-heavy, commute-sensitive, HOA-sensitive, school-zone-sensitive, summer-utility-sensitive, and strongly influenced by North Las Vegas, northeast Las Vegas, Aliante, Centennial Hills, Summerlin, and Henderson housing decisions.
base_profile.base_bluf: Nellis is not just a Las Vegas housing decision. It is a BAH-fit, commute, school-zone, HOA, summer-utility, assignment-length, and neighborhood-screening decision. The cooling market creates leverage, but buyers still need disciplined all-in payment math.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, commute testing, childcare access, safety screening, and total monthly housing cost before signing near Nellis.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, rental flexibility, roommate options, and access to daily services around Nellis and North Las Vegas.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Nellis housing conversations are about BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, HOA exposure, summer utilities, and all-in payment math.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Nellis is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, Las Vegas rental depth, and practical commute corridors.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Nellis AFB / Northeast Las Vegas
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Nellis AFB / Northeast Las Vegas can offer practical commute access, but users must screen by exact neighborhood, safety, school zones, and property condition.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on exact address, gate, traffic, and report time
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Neighborhood quality can vary materially
School assignment should be verified by exact address
Property condition and safety should be screened carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong for commute-first users when the exact property, safety profile, and monthly cost are sound.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: North Las Vegas
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: North Las Vegas is a common Nellis PCS research corridor with practical commute options, newer subdivisions in some areas, and mixed neighborhood profiles.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-30 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Subdivision quality and HOA exposure vary
School zones must be verified by exact address
Commute should be tested during expected report hours
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for many Nellis households because it can balance commute, price, and housing choice.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Aliante
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Aliante can offer family appeal, planned-community housing, and stronger lifestyle fit, but HOA and commute tradeoffs should be reviewed.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Commuter Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: HOA exposure should be included in all-in payment
Higher prices can pressure affordability
Commute should be tested during report time
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong candidate for families if HOA, commute, and all-in payment remain inside the safe lane.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Centennial Hills
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Centennial Hills can offer northwest Las Vegas lifestyle appeal and family-oriented housing, but it may add commute and price tradeoffs.

Buyers prioritizing services and neighborhood feel

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Mixed compared with many military markets because close-in options exist, but Las Vegas traffic, gate access, shift schedules, school drop-off timing, and exact address can materially change the daily commute.
primary_commute_risks: Choosing by mileage instead of report-time commute
Traffic from North Las Vegas, Aliante, Centennial Hills, Summerlin, Henderson, and east-side corridors
Longer drives from premium lifestyle corridors
planning_guidance: Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Confirm which gate best supports the user’s duty location.
Compare North Las Vegas, northeast Las Vegas, Aliante, Centennial Hills, Sunrise Manor, Summerlin, and Henderson by route, services, schools, safety, HOA, and resale.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: For Nellis, gate access and traffic timing often matter more than straight-line distance. The best housing choice balances BAH fit, commute, schools, HOA, summer utilities, and assignment length.

bah_market_reality.overall_position: Mixed for buyers and moderate for renters, with cooling-market opportunity.
bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: Cooling prices can improve buyer leverage, but Las Vegas purchase prices, HOA dues, insurance, and summer utility costs can still pressure many BAH profiles.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for shorter assignments, users with tight residual income, or households that want flexibility while learning the Las Vegas metro.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Nellis should be modeled as a cooling but still payment-sensitive market. PCSUnited should test each user’s rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, timeline, family needs, commute tolerance, HOA exposure, and exit plan before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Access to services, shopping, sports, healthcare, and entertainment
On-base housing can simplify the PCS for some families
watchouts: School assignments and program fit should be verified by exact address
Childcare availability and waitlists should be checked early
Summer heat and utility costs can affect daily life and monthly budget
Commute and traffic can matter more than expected
Neighborhood safety and property condition vary sharply across the metro
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Nellis can work well for families who want large-metro options and strong military infrastructure, but it requires disciplined screening for schools, safety, commute, HOA, and all-in payment.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Nellis AFB sources.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.
Compare North Las Vegas, northeast Las Vegas, Aliante, Centennial Hills, Sunrise Manor, Summerlin, Henderson, and other corridors using commute, schools, safety, services, and all-in monthly cost.
Review taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, property condition, and commute route before buying.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Test the commute during the expected report-time window before signing.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not assume Las Vegas affordability from purchase price alone.
Do not choose only by distance to Nellis.
Do not ignore HOA dues, summer cooling costs, insurance, utilities, and maintenance.
Verify schools, commute, safety, and property condition by exact address before committing.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
Cooling markets still require strong residual-income discipline.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with BAH realism, commute testing, monthly payment discipline, school-zone verification, HOA screening, summer-utility stress testing, and exit strategy. Nellis clients need grounded housing math, not generic Las Vegas market advice.
strong_listing_angles: Low or reasonable HOA when applicable
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, cooling costs, and maintenance
North Las Vegas versus Aliante versus Summerlin versus Henderson lifestyle tradeoffs
Property condition and VA appraisal readiness
Future resale appeal to military buyers
investor_consultation_angles: HOA, insurance, utility, and maintenance reserves
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Nellis realtor should sound like a BAH strategist, commute filter, HOA screener, school-zone guide, and cooling-market risk manager — not just a sales agent.

notes: Market and housing values reflect July 2026 RentCast market data across selected Nellis PCS housing ZIPs.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Nellis base orientation map is created.
Nellis market guidance should account for North Las Vegas, northeast Las Vegas, Aliante, Centennial Hills, Sunrise Manor, Summerlin, Henderson, and broader Las Vegas housing pressure.

source_notes: Use official Nellis AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Nellis housing, Air Force Housing, and privatized housing sources for on-base housing and housing-office details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use RentCast market data, Census demographic data, MLS-style housing data, and local rental listing observations for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.
